data.title: Bogota Financial (BSBK) Net Interest Margin Rebound Tests Bearish Earnings Narratives
data.description:
Bogota Financial (BSBK) has just posted another profitable quarter, with Q3 FY 2025 revenue of US$4.3 million and basic EPS of US$0.04 supported by net income of US$0.5 million and a net interest margin of 1.8% as investors keep an eye on how sustainable this run rate looks. Over recent periods, the company has seen revenue move from US$3.0 million in Q2 FY 2024 to US$4.6 million in Q1 FY 2025 and US$4.0 million in Q2 FY 2025, while basic EPS shifted from a loss of US$0.03 in Q2 FY 2024 to a profit of US$0.06 in Q1 FY 2025 and US$0.02 in Q2 FY 2025. This gives a clearer line of sight on how profitability is rebuilding. With that backdrop, the latest results put the spotlight firmly on how margins are holding up as the bank balances growth with credit quality and funding costs.

See our full analysis for Bogota Financial.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to compare this earnings profile against the most widely held narratives around Bogota Financial to see which views the latest results support and which might need a rethink.

Curious how numbers become stories that shape markets? Explore Community Narratives

Advertisement

1.8% net interest margin with shrinking loan book

  • Net interest margin sits at 1.8% in Q3 FY 2025, with total loans at US$671.8 million compared with US$704.1 million in Q1 FY 2025 and US$714.3 million in Q4 FY 2024.
  • What stands out for the bullish narrative that focuses on high quality earnings is that the recent profitability sits alongside a smaller loan base, as net income moved from a loss of US$0.9 million in Q4 FY 2024 to a profit of US$0.7 million in Q1 FY 2025 and US$0.5 million in Q3 FY 2025. Over the same period, net interest margin moved from 1.16% on a trailing twelve month basis in Q4 FY 2024 to quarterly readings between 1.66% and 1.8% in FY 2025 so far.
    • Supporters who point to a cleaner earnings profile can reference that the bank became profitable over the last year and is described as having high quality past earnings, even as total loans have stepped down from above US$710 million in mid 2024 to below US$680 million in Q3 FY 2025.
    • At the same time, five year trailing earnings have declined by 47.8% per year, so the recent margin and profit figures sit against a longer record of weaker profitability that bullish investors need to weigh carefully.

Profitable again, but five year earnings down 47.8% annually

  • On a trailing twelve month view, the bank reports it has returned to profitability with net income of US$0.5 million in the period to Q3 FY 2025, compared with losses of US$2.4 million in the period to Q3 FY 2024 and US$2.2 million in the period to Q4 FY 2024, while five year trailing earnings have declined at an annualized rate of 47.8%.
  • Bears focus on that 47.8% annual earnings decline over five years and argue that the recent profit may not fully offset a weak longer term record. The numbers give them some backing because trailing twelve month basic EPS moved from a loss per share of US$0.19 in the period to Q3 FY 2024 to a profit of US$0.04 in the period to Q3 FY 2025, which is an improvement but still small in absolute terms.
    • Critics highlight that while quarterly basic EPS has swung from a loss of US$0.07 in Q4 FY 2024 to profits of US$0.06 in Q1 FY 2025 and US$0.04 in Q3 FY 2025, the cumulative five year decline suggests past profitability was materially higher than what the bank is currently generating.
    • For cautious investors, that combination of a long run 47.8% annualized earnings drop alongside only modest trailing twelve month profit can make the recent turnaround feel more like a repair phase than a return to earlier earnings power.
If you are trying to decide whether this rebound really changes the story, it helps to see how optimists and skeptics frame it side by side before you make up your own mind. 📊 Read the full Bogota Financial Consensus Narrative.

3% non performing loans with 12% allowance coverage

  • Non performing loans are reported at 3% of the book, treated here as high, while the allowance for bad loans covers 12% of those non performing balances, which is described as low. On the balance sheet that shows up as non performing loans of US$20.5 million in Q3 FY 2025 compared with US$13.9 million in Q1 FY 2025 and US$13.0 million in Q2 FY 2024.
  • Skeptical investors point to these credit figures as a key risk, since a higher 3% bad loan ratio together with only 12% allowance coverage can pressure future profitability. The quarterly pattern shows non performing loans rising from around US$13.8 million in Q3 FY 2024 to US$20.5 million in Q3 FY 2025 even as the bank has just turned profitable over the last year.

data.title: Astra Microwave Products Limited Just Missed Earnings And Its Revenue Numbers Were Weaker Than Expected
data.description:
The third-quarter results for Astra Microwave Products Limited (NSE:ASTRAMICRO) were released last week, making it a good time to revisit its performance. Results look mixed - while revenue fell marginally short of analyst estimates at ₹2.6b, statutory earnings were in line with expectations, at ₹16.17 per share. This is an important time for investors, as they can track a company's performance in its report, look at what experts are forecasting for next year, and see if there has been any change to expectations for the business. We thought readers would find it interesting to see the analysts latest (statutory) post-earnings forecasts for next year.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

Taking into account the latest results, the current consensus from Astra Microwave Products' five analysts is for revenues of ₹14.0b in 2027. This would reflect a substantial 29% increase on its revenue over the past 12 months. Statutory earnings per share are predicted to bounce 38% to ₹22.90. Yet prior to the latest earnings, the analysts had been anticipated revenues of ₹14.6b and earnings per share (EPS) of ₹23.53 in 2027. The analysts are less bullish than they were before these results, given the reduced revenue forecasts and the small dip in earnings per share expectations.

View our latest analysis for Astra Microwave Products

Despite the cuts to forecast earnings, there was no real change to the ₹1,170 price target, showing that the analysts don't think the changes have a meaningful impact on its intrinsic value. That's not the only conclusion we can draw from this data however, as some investors also like to consider the spread in estimates when evaluating analyst price targets. The most optimistic Astra Microwave Products analyst has a price target of ₹1,452 per share, while the most pessimistic values it at ₹1,028. Analysts definitely have varying views on the business, but the spread of estimates is not wide enough in our view to suggest that extreme outcomes could await Astra Microwave Products shareholders.

These estimates are interesting, but it can be useful to paint some more broad strokes when seeing how forecasts compare, both to the Astra Microwave Products' past performance and to peers in the same industry. It's clear from the latest estimates that Astra Microwave Products' rate of growth is expected to accelerate meaningfully, with the forecast 23% annualised revenue growth to the end of 2027 noticeably faster than its historical growth of 11% p.a. over the past five years. Compare this with other companies in the same industry, which are forecast to see revenue growth of 29% annually. So it's clear that despite the acceleration in growth, Astra Microwave Products is expected to grow meaningfully slower than the industry average.

Advertisement

The Bottom Line

The biggest concern is that the analysts reduced their earnings per share estimates, suggesting business headwinds could lay ahead for Astra Microwave Products. Unfortunately, they also downgraded their revenue estimates, and our data indicates underperformance compared to the wider industry. Even so, earnings per share are more important to the intrinsic value of the business. The consensus price target held steady at ₹1,170, with the latest estimates not enough to have an impact on their price targets.

With that in mind, we wouldn't be too quick to come to a conclusion on Astra Microwave Products. Long-term earnings power is much more important than next year's profits. data.title: Investors Should Be Encouraged By Bharat Electronics' (NSE:BEL) Returns On Capital
data.description:
To find a multi-bagger stock, what are the underlying trends we should look for in a business? One common approach is to try and find a company with returns on capital employed (ROCE) that are increasing, in conjunction with a growing amount of capital employed. This shows us that it's a compounding machine, able to continually reinvest its earnings back into the business and generate higher returns. So when we looked at the ROCE trend of Bharat Electronics (NSE:BEL) we really liked what we saw.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

Advertisement

Understanding Return On Capital Employed (ROCE)

For those that aren't sure what ROCE is, it measures the amount of pre-tax profits a company can generate from the capital employed in its business. The formula for this calculation on Bharat Electronics is:

Return on Capital Employed = Earnings Before Interest and Tax (EBIT) ÷ (Total Assets - Current Liabilities)

0.32 = ₹74b ÷ (₹429b - ₹202b) (Based on the trailing twelve months to December 2025).

Thus, Bharat Electronics has an ROCE of 32%. That's a fantastic return and not only that, it outpaces the average of 17% earned by companies in a similar industry.

See our latest analysis for Bharat Electronics

Above you can see how the current ROCE for Bharat Electronics compares to its prior returns on capital, but there's only so much you can tell from the past. If you're interested, you can view the analysts predictions in our free analyst report for Bharat Electronics .

What Can We Tell From Bharat Electronics' ROCE Trend?

Bharat Electronics is displaying some positive trends. Over the last five years, returns on capital employed have risen substantially to 32%. The company is effectively making more money per dollar of capital used, and it's worth noting that the amount of capital has increased too, by 95%. So we're very much inspired by what we're seeing at Bharat Electronics thanks to its ability to profitably reinvest capital.

Another thing to note, Bharat Electronics has a high ratio of current liabilities to total assets of 47%. This effectively means that suppliers (or short-term creditors) are funding a large portion of the business, so just be aware that this can introduce some elements of risk. Ideally we'd like to see this reduce as that would mean fewer obligations bearing risks.

The Bottom Line On Bharat Electronics' ROCE

To sum it up, Bharat Electronics has proven it can reinvest in the business and generate higher returns on that capital employed, which is terrific. Since the stock has returned a staggering 938% to shareholders over the last five years, it looks like investors are recognizing these changes. Therefore, we think it would be worth your time to check if these trends are going to continue.

Bharat Electronics does have some risks though, and we've spotted 1 warning sign for Bharat Electronics that you might be interested in.

data.title: Return Trends At Birlasoft (NSE:BSOFT) Aren't Appealing
data.description:
If you're looking for a multi-bagger, there's a few things to keep an eye out for. One common approach is to try and find a company with returns on capital employed (ROCE) that are increasing, in conjunction with a growing amount of capital employed. If you see this, it typically means it's a company with a great business model and plenty of profitable reinvestment opportunities. So, when we ran our eye over Birlasoft's (NSE:BSOFT) trend of ROCE, we liked what we saw.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

Return On Capital Employed (ROCE): What Is It?

Just to clarify if you're unsure, ROCE is a metric for evaluating how much pre-tax income (in percentage terms) a company earns on the capital invested in its business. The formula for this calculation on Birlasoft is:

Return on Capital Employed = Earnings Before Interest and Tax (EBIT) ÷ (Total Assets - Current Liabilities)

0.18 = ₹7.2b ÷ (₹48b - ₹8.4b) (Based on the trailing twelve months to December 2025).

Thus, Birlasoft has an ROCE of 18%. On its own, that's a standard return, however it's much better than the 12% generated by the Software industry.

Above you can see how the current ROCE for Birlasoft compares to its prior returns on capital, but there's only so much you can tell from the past. If you'd like to see what analysts are forecasting going forward, you should check out our free analyst report for Birlasoft .

What Does the ROCE Trend For Birlasoft Tell Us?

While the current returns on capital are decent, they haven't changed much. Over the past five years, ROCE has remained relatively flat at around 18% and the business has deployed 78% more capital into its operations. 18% is a pretty standard return, and it provides some comfort knowing that Birlasoft has consistently earned this amount. Stable returns in this ballpark can be unexciting, but if they can be maintained over the long run, they often provide nice rewards to shareholders.

The Key Takeaway

To sum it up, Birlasoft has simply been reinvesting capital steadily, at those decent rates of return. And since the stock has risen strongly over the last five years, it appears the market might expect this trend to continue. So while investors seem to be recognizing these promising trends, we still believe the stock deserves further research.

data.title: LT Foods Limited's (NSE:LTFOODS) Stock Is Going Strong: Is the Market Following Fundamentals?
data.description:
LT Foods (NSE:LTFOODS) has had a great run on the share market with its stock up by a significant 17% over the last month. Given that the market rewards strong financials in the long-term, we wonder if that is the case in this instance. In this article, we decided to focus on LT Foods' ROE.

Return on Equity or ROE is a test of how effectively a company is growing its value and managing investors’ money. Put another way, it reveals the company's success at turning shareholder investments into profits.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

Advertisement

How Is ROE Calculated?

Return on equity can be calculated by using the formula:

Return on Equity = Net Profit (from continuing operations) ÷ Shareholders' Equity

So, based on the above formula, the ROE for LT Foods is:

16% = ₹6.5b ÷ ₹42b (Based on the trailing twelve months to December 2025).

The 'return' is the income the business earned over the last year. So, this means that for every ₹1 of its shareholder's investments, the company generates a profit of ₹0.16.

View our latest analysis for LT Foods

Why Is ROE Important For Earnings Growth?

So far, we've learned that ROE is a measure of a company's profitability. Based on how much of its profits the company chooses to reinvest or "retain", we are then able to evaluate a company's future ability to generate profits. Generally speaking, other things being equal, firms with a high return on equity and profit retention, have a higher growth rate than firms that don’t share these attributes.

LT Foods' Earnings Growth And 16% ROE

To start with, LT Foods' ROE looks acceptable. On comparing with the average industry ROE of 11% the company's ROE looks pretty remarkable. This probably laid the ground for LT Foods' significant 21% net income growth seen over the past five years. However, there could also be other causes behind this growth. For instance, the company has a low payout ratio or is being managed efficiently.

As a next step, we compared LT Foods' net income growth with the industry and found that the company has a similar growth figure when compared with the industry average growth rate of 20% in the same period.

Earnings growth is an important metric to consider when valuing a stock. What investors need to determine next is if the expected earnings growth, or the lack of it, is already built into the share price. This then helps them determine if the stock is placed for a bright or bleak future. One good indicator of expected earnings growth is the P/E ratio which determines the price the market is willing to pay for a stock based on its earnings prospects. So, you may want to check if LT Foods is trading on a high P/E or a low P/E, relative to its industry.

Is LT Foods Using Its Retained Earnings Effectively?

LT Foods has a really low three-year median payout ratio of 9.3%, meaning that it has the remaining 91% left over to reinvest into its business. So it looks like LT Foods is reinvesting profits heavily to grow its business, which shows in its earnings growth.

Moreover, LT Foods is determined to keep sharing its profits with shareholders which we infer from its long history of paying a dividend for at least ten years. Looking at the current analyst consensus data, we can see that the company's future payout ratio is expected to rise to 11% over the next three years. Regardless, the ROE is not expected to change much for the company despite the higher expected payout ratio.

Conclusion

Overall, we are quite pleased with LT Foods' performance. Particularly, we like that the company is reinvesting heavily into its business, and at a high rate of return. Unsurprisingly, this has led to an impressive earnings growth. The latest industry analyst forecasts show that the company is expected to maintain its current growth rate. data.title: We Think Bharti Airtel's (NSE:BHARTIARTL) Solid Earnings Are Understated
data.description:
Bharti Airtel Limited's (NSE:BHARTIARTL) recent earnings report didn't offer any surprises, with the shares unchanged over the last week. We did some analysis to find out why and believe that investors might be missing some encouraging factors contained in the earnings.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

Advertisement

Examining Cashflow Against Bharti Airtel's Earnings

In high finance, the key ratio used to measure how well a company converts reported profits into free cash flow (FCF) is the accrual ratio (from cashflow). To get the accrual ratio we first subtract FCF from profit for a period, and then divide that number by the average operating assets for the period. This ratio tells us how much of a company's profit is not backed by free cashflow.

That means a negative accrual ratio is a good thing, because it shows that the company is bringing in more free cash flow than its profit would suggest. That is not intended to imply we should worry about a positive accrual ratio, but it's worth noting where the accrual ratio is rather high. That's because some academic studies have suggested that high accruals ratios tend to lead to lower profit or less profit growth.

Bharti Airtel has an accrual ratio of -0.14 for the year to December 2025. That implies it has good cash conversion, and implies that its free cash flow solidly exceeded its profit last year. In fact, it had free cash flow of ₹685b in the last year, which was a lot more than its statutory profit of ₹303.9b. Bharti Airtel's free cash flow improved over the last year, which is generally good to see.

That might leave you wondering what analysts are forecasting in terms of future profitability. Luckily, you can click here to see an interactive graph depicting future profitability, based on their estimates.

Our Take On Bharti Airtel's Profit Performance

Bharti Airtel's accrual ratio is solid, and indicates strong free cash flow, as we discussed, above. Based on this observation, we consider it likely that Bharti Airtel's statutory profit actually understates its earnings potential! Better yet, its EPS are growing strongly, which is nice to see. At the end of the day, it's essential to consider more than just the factors above, if you want to understand the company properly. With this in mind, we wouldn't consider investing in a stock unless we had a thorough understanding of the risks. In terms of investment risks, we've identified 2 warning signs with Bharti Airtel, and understanding them should be part of your investment process.

data.description:
The board of NATCO Pharma Limited (NSE:NATCOPHARM) has announced that it will pay a dividend on the 14th of March, with investors receiving ₹1.50 per share. The dividend yield is 0.6% based on this payment, which is a little bit low compared to the other companies in the industry.

NATCO Pharma's Future Dividend Projections Appear Well Covered By Earnings

The dividend yield is a little bit low, but sustainability of the payments is also an important part of evaluating an income stock. However, prior to this announcement, NATCO Pharma's dividend was comfortably covered by both cash flow and earnings. This means that most of its earnings are being retained to grow the business.

Looking forward, earnings per share is forecast to fall by 71.8% over the next year. If the dividend continues along the path it has been on recently, we estimate the payout ratio could be 21%, which is comfortable for the company to continue in the future.

Dividend Volatility

While the company has been paying a dividend for a long time, it has cut the dividend at least once in the last 10 years. Since 2016, the dividend has gone from ₹1.00 total annually to ₹5.00. This works out to be a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of approximately 17% a year over that time. It is great to see strong growth in the dividend payments, but cuts are concerning as it may indicate the payout policy is too ambitious.

The Dividend Looks Likely To Grow

Given that the dividend has been cut in the past, we need to check if earnings are growing and if that might lead to stronger dividends in the future. NATCO Pharma has impressed us by growing EPS at 27% per year over the past five years. A low payout ratio gives the company a lot of flexibility, and growing earnings also make it very easy for it to grow the dividend.

We Really Like NATCO Pharma's Dividend

In summary, it is good to see that the dividend is staying consistent, and we don't think there is any reason to suspect this might change over the medium term. The company is generating plenty of cash, and the earnings also quite easily cover the distributions. However, it is worth noting that the earnings are expected to fall over the next year, which may not change the long term outlook, but could affect the dividend payment in the next 12 months. All of these factors considered, we think this has solid potential as a dividend stock.
